verbatim_text: Is this something that, if you have many people who are running loads like this, you would see simultaneous updates to the model weights from everybody, or you get your own copy of the model, and you have to keep this billion-parameter model sort of separate from everybody else's copy? How do you think about that piece of it? Yeah, this is very interesting. So, you know, if you take an open-source model, just because that's the one I can actually closely observe, such as 1.8b or something, and you run it on 128,000 contexts. So context, you have to update this on a per-user basis, and you measure how long is the list. That's the KV cache that I was showing for attention. And you add up all the KV cache. You see that it actually, just for 128,000 context, it gets to about a billion. Now, if you have 10 times that amount, or like 100 times that amount, you will actually end up having to carry around 100 billion worth of KV cache per user. That's actually much larger than the model itself. So, I think the idea of carrying a model around for each user, a billion model in this case, it does sound a little unreal. The only thing we need to realize is that the baseline now, without doing this, is much more unreal actually. It's just behind the hood. So, it's really that fear of updating your model.
